file_insights: Created comprehensive DAO classes with 1,818 total lines of code
Implemented trip_dao.dart (418 lines) with CRUD operations for Trip entity
Implemented journal_dao.dart (499 lines) with CRUD operations for Journal entity
Implemented sync_queue_dao.dart (443 lines) for sync queue management
Implemented user_dao.dart (442 lines) for user profile operations
Created offline_dao.dart (15 lines) as barrel export for all DAOs
Updated multiple auto-claude tracking files for progress management
Updated build-progress.txt to mark subtask 2.4 as completed
Updated implementation_plan.json with detailed notes and status
patterns_discovered: Repository pattern with abstract base classes for each entity type
Transaction pattern for batch operations and atomic database operations
Query builder pattern for flexible filtering and pagination
Soft delete pattern with version tracking for offline-first architecture
Sync-aware query pattern with methods like getUnsynced*() and mark*AsSynced()
Batch operation pattern for bulk inserts, updates, and cleanup
Barrel export pattern for clean module organization
Version increment pattern for conflict resolution
gotchas_discovered: Non-monotonic Git index errors encountered during commit operations (previously)
Need for comprehensive error handling in database operations
Complex query building for offline-first scenarios with sync status tracking
Proper handling of soft deletes vs hard deletes in data layer
Thread safety considerations for concurrent database operations
Proper transaction management to prevent data corruption

approach_outcome.approach_used: Implemented comprehensive DAO classes following repository pattern with abstract base classes, transaction support, and sync-aware queries for offline-first architecture
approach_outcome.why_it_worked: Followed established repository pattern, implemented comprehensive CRUD operations, added sync-aware queries for offline scenarios, included transaction support for batch operations, and maintained clean barrel export organization

recommendations: Consider adding unit tests for all DAO classes to ensure data integrity
Implement database migration support for schema changes in future iterations
Add comprehensive error logging and monitoring for database operations
Consider implementing connection pooling for high-concurrency scenarios
Add performance monitoring for complex queries and batch operations
Implement database backup and restore functionality for disaster recovery
Consider adding encryption for sensitive data at the database layer
